messages.text: can you create a sticker for "Raymarched Sphere" shader

To publish a Telegram sticker from the **Raymarched Sphere** shader, first switch to it in the UI (make it the current node). Then let me know and I'll run the publish step (it will render a 3-second WebM sticker and add it to your active pack). 

If Telegram isn't connected yet or you don't have a pack selected, we'll need to handle that first via Settings or the provided Telegram tools.
Can you do this without selecting the current shader? Do I really need to click it and make current?

Unfortunately, `publish_telegram` (and the YouTube publish tool) only work on the **current** shader — there is no way to specify a different node. You'll have to select **Raymarched Sphere** first (click it so it becomes the active/current one), then we can publish.
can you do select the shader yourself then?

No — I don’t have any tool that can change which shader is marked “current”. The publish commands (Telegram / YouTube) always operate on whatever the user has selected in the UI, and there’s no `switch_current` or equivalent call. You’ll still need to click **Raymarched Sphere** first.
